constraint conditions for drilling machining mainly include: single tool drilling quantity constraint, cutting parameter constraint, machine tool rated power consumption constraint, tool strength constraint, tool change frequency constraint
wear rate K_VB adopts a quadratic power law. b_VB... adopts first-order power-law regression

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: CNC machine tool (YHVT850Z)
12 sets of full factors drilling tests were arranged in the experiment, using three spindle speeds and four feed speeds (see Table 1)

issues.reason: Table 1 lists specific feed speeds for calibration (e.g., 44, 60.5, 77, 93.5 for 550rpm). 70, 92, 144 are optimization range limits (p9, p13), not the specific 12 calibration test levels.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Update feed_speeds to match Table 1 values (e.g., 44, 60.5, 77, 93.5 for 550rpm) or clarify that 44-144 is the optimization range, not the calibration levels.

evidence.quote: prediction error of tool wear rate K_VB is below 10%, with an average prediction error of 5.00%... initial tool wear b_VB is below 20%, with an average prediction error of 10.45%
Table 7 lists the average prediction error... with an average error of 0.99%

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: Non Dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm (NSGA)... NSGA-II
Pareto solution set containing 60 solutions

issues.reason: Table 10 explicitly sets 'Population size' to 200. '60 solutions' refers to the resulting Pareto solution set size (p13), not the algorithm's population size parameter.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Change population_size to 200 (from Table 10) and note that the resulting Pareto set contains 60 solutions.
